title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
summary: Aluminum prices remained flat globally at $3,461.28/Ton and ¥23,822/Ton in China, with a 0.00% daily change as markets consolidate following recent severe supply shocks. Primary drivers include Iranian missile strikes damaging Emirates Global Aluminium and Alba smelters, removing critical capacity, compounded by the Strait of Hormuz closure paralyzing Gulf exports and causing LME warehouse inventories to plunge. Analysts project a 570,000-tonne global deficit, exposing the market to significant upside risk.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum fell to 3,557 USD/T on April 17, 2026, down 2.40% from the previous day, with UK futures sinking to $3,550 per tonne from a three-year high of $3,670 on April 16th. Signals of restored vessel flows through the Strait of Hormuz improved the outlook, as Iranian authorities stated commercial vessels are now free to navigate following talks with the US. Over the past month, prices have risen 4.15% and are up 48.55% year-over-year, with expectations to reach 3600.28 USD/Tonne by quarter-end.

title: It's not just oil — the Iran war is disrupting helium and aluminum ...
summary: The Iran war is disrupting global supplies of helium and aluminum, critical materials for semiconductors, medical equipment, and everyday goods. Gulf countries produce roughly 9% of the world's aluminum, and local disruptions from Iranian strikes are reducing supply while raising energy costs and production expenses. Aluminum prices reached a four-year high this week, tightening market conditions according to Oxford Economics experts.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum prices fell to 3557 USD/T on April 17, 2026, down 2.40% from the previous day, despite a 4.15% monthly rise and 48.55% yearly increase. Emirates Global Aluminium, the largest producer, declared force majeure on some deliveries after halting operations at its Al Taweelah smelter due to Iranian strikes.

title: Steel prices, news & market analysis
summary: Italian and German steel mills signaled to buyers at a major industry event in Dusseldorf that they will increase offers by €50-70/t next week. German rebar producers indicated offers around €710/t delivered, €50/t higher than recent levels. Italian mills noted offers higher than €50/t following last week's €680/t ex-works price.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel fell to 3,097 CNY/T on April 17, 2026, down 0.19% from the previous day, with a 1.28% monthly decline but 2.45% higher year-over-year. Steel rebar futures climbed above CNY 3,100 per ton on firm seasonal demand and restocking. China Steel Corp. raised domestic prices for next month by NT$1,000 to NT$1,200 per ton.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel rose to 1,105.09 USD/T on April 20, 2026, up 0.28% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have risen 5.45%, and are up 17.56% year-over-year.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart follows recent operational disruptions at the site.

title: Ivanhoe Mines Reports 71,417 Tonnes of Copper in Anode Produced by Kamoa-Kakula in Q1 2026; Recovery Efforts Advancing
summary: Ivanhoe Mines announced production of 71,417 tonnes of copper in anode at the Kamoa-Kakula Copper Complex during Q1 2026. Africa's largest copper smelter has ramped up to 60% capacity, with improving sales of 99.7%-pure anodes and high-strength sulphuric acid. The Phase 1, 2, and 3 concentrators are set to reach a combined steady-state rate of 17 million tonnes per annum, with revised guidance for over 500,000 tonnes of copper per annum from 2028.

title: Copper Market News & Forecasts
summary: Copper prices are at record highs amid warnings of unsustainable deficits, with key trends including offtake deals and M&A to secure supply. Mitsui & Co acquired 40% offtake rights to Argentina’s Josemaria deposit, while Fortescue completed acquisition of Peru’s Cañariaco project. Roughly 40,000 tonnes per month of copper cathode face rerouting challenges after the Strait of Hormuz closure.

title: Global Copper Output Decline Sparks Industrial Supply Chain Crisis
summary: The global copper market faces a significant output decline threatening industrial supply chains worldwide. This shortage is reshaping supply dynamics amid rising demand.

title: Indonesia nickel processing utilization to drop amid lower nickel ore
summary: Indonesia's nickel industry group FINI stated that 2026 nickel processing production capacity utilization could drop to as low as 70% due to a lower nickel ore production quota. Nickel ore imports are expected to surge as a result. Utilization is projected to fall to 70-75% from 90% last year.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, has been notified of a drastic output cut to 12 million tons of ore production quota for this year, down from 42 million tons in 2025. This move aims to boost global prices of nickel, Indonesia's key export. The company intends to apply for a revision.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: Indonesia announced a 2026 nickel ore production target of 250-260 million tons, flat-to-down from 2025 levels, supporting nickel prices toward $20,000 per ton amid surging EV demand. EV markets show 20% underlying growth, with nickel consumption projected to grow 6-7% annually. Analysts await confirmation of Indonesian production discipline.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: Indonesia plans to cut nickel output in 2026 to support prices and government revenue, reversing mining quota policies to regulate supply volatility. The policy evolution has caused market confusion but positions the country to capitalize on volatility. Minister Bahlil Lahadalia confirmed the plans on Dec. 19.
